title: Russian authorities ask residents to work from home as fuel crisis escalates
summary: Citizens were also told to limit their use of private vehicles, according to local governments.
imageAlt: Russian authorities ask residents to work from home as fuel crisis escalates
aiSummary: Citizens were also told to limit their use of private vehicles, according to local governments.
keyPoints: Russian authorities ask residents to work from home as fuel crisis escalates
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de internacional.

multimedia.alt: Russian authorities ask residents to work from home as fuel crisis escalates
multimedia.caption: Imagen de portada del artículo
Cars queue at a gas station operated by Rosneft, a state-controlled Russian oil  company, on June 27, 2026, in Moscow. | Contributor/Getty Images

fullArticle:
Russia’s Siberian government officials are telling businesses to shift to remote work when possible and limit energy consumption, according to local media reports.

“Officials are … advised to plan official trips more selectively and make wider use of remote interaction formats (such as conducting meetings online),” said Olga Krylova, the deputy governor of Siberia’s Tomsk region.

In Novosibirsk, another Siberian region, Deputy Governor Konstantin Khalzov signed a decree recommending all businesses let employees work remotely, with the exception of essential workers.

The decree also encourages citizens to use their cars as little as possible. “We recommend that residents of the Novosibirsk region limit the use of private vehicles. If travel is necessary, they should ensure they have enou...
